purpose: The commit fixes a bug in the hci_cmd_sync_queue function by returning an error when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set.
The commit removes an unnecessary header file inclusion (asm/termios.h) in a kernel module source code (n_hdlc.c).
The commit updates documentation for store metric 'N/A', which is a new feature that adds a new column to the report table.
The commit fixes a bug in the hugetlb vma lock handling during split vma and range unmapping.
The commit adds two new supplies (AVDD and MICVDD) to an existing codec driver for a Realtek audio device.
The commit fixes a resource leak in zorro7xx_remove_one() by adding an iounmap() call to undo the ioremap() done during probe.
The commit removes an unused field from a struct in the net/mlx5e module.
The commit changes the type of cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx from unsigned to signed.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in defconfigs for RISC-V, which should have no functional change but will make the code easier to read and maintain.
The commit introduces a new function to check if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode.
The commit is related to the RTC (Real-Time Clock) device driver in Linux. It adds a new test case for the rtc_valid_tm() function, which checks if a given time structure is valid or not.
The commit removes redundant assignment to a variable 'bytes' in the echoaudio driver.
The commit decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks, allowing a kernel built without RCU Tasks to still use rcuscale.
The commit removes duplicated text in a comment.
The commit message explains that the change is a revert of a previous change that caused an underflow issue. The code changes are not explained in detail.
The commit adds a new compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC, which has a Bifrost Mali-G31 GPU.
The commit renames a struct to make it more consistent with other names in the driver.
The commit fixes a bug in the amd-xgbe driver by changing the format specifier for printing a u16 value from %#06hx to %x.
The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS jump_label implementation by changing the type of the branch range check to long to perform a signed comparison.
The commit removes a case from SetHwReg8188EU() that calls a function. The change is part of an ongoing effort to remove the unwanted hal layer.
The commit declares non-global symbols as static to fix sparse warnings introduced while adding errata.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from display/msm nodes.
The commit adds a new feature to the media subsystem by allocating arrays in struct ctrl_ref.
The commit adds new information to the socinfo driver for several PMICs and SoCs.
The commit adds support for new chip cuts (A-P) in the rtl8xxxu driver.
The commit removes a custom software enable count from PRCMU clocks in the UX500 platform.

The commit is related to the initialization of the memory management system for RISC-V processors. It removes unnecessary code related to the CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL configuration option and groups together declarations and definitions that were previously separated by this option.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to support I2C nodes.

The commit fixes a bug in the thermal driver for RZG2L by replacing a comment that does not explain much with a more accurate description of the total number of ADC data samples. It also fixes a typo and improves code style.
The commit fixes an infinite loop that may occur if we fail to acquire the HW semaphore needed for resource release.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology for clocking.
The commit removes an unnecessary hack that was used to define IS_SWAPFILE.
The commit adds a new feature to the Linux kernel by implementing GTP echo responses.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate. It also creates a new function called cpumask_first_zero() that is an analogue of the 'next' version if n == -1.
The commit removes a call to an IOVAR that is no longer supported in the firmware, which would cause a failure message to be displayed on the console.
The commit adds support for a new platform by adding a board configuration file and connecting the alc5682 headset codec to SSP0.
The commit adds a new feature to the io_uring subsystem: support for zero-copy sendmsg() operations on network sockets.
The commit adds a test to ensure that the xeCJK.sty file exists before loading it.
The commit adds a new feature to checkpatch.pl that allows macros with complex values to be enclosed in parentheses.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with atomic helpers to improve code architecture and enable easier code sharing among drivers.
The commit adds a new interface for jpeg decoding workers to ensure that three HWs run in parallel in MT8195.

The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls from libata, which is a part of the Linux kernel's ATA subsystem. The changes are primarily focused on improving performance and reducing clutter in the codebase.
The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank, which is a feature that allows the display to enter a low-power state when not displaying content.
The commit fixes a type mismatch in the hqd_destroy() callback function, which caused a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure in the kernel.
The commit renames a property in a binding file from 'reset' to 'resets', which is the correct name according to the documentation.
The commit fixes a warning in the GCC compiler by relegating it to W=1+.
The commit changes the way the sun8i-ss driver uses the pm_runtime_resume_and_get function to simplify code.
The commit splits out fixed file installation and removal from io_uring and moves it to a new file called filetable. It also changes the helpers to take a ctx rather than an io_kiocb.
The commit adds two new device tree nodes for G1 and G2 decoders, which are independent blocks that can be fused out or enabled by the vpu-blk-ctrl power-domain controller.
The commit removes a macro that was only used once in the codebase.
